title: Low complexity encryption with fountain codes
abstract: Fountain codes require side information for decoding. In an example, the use of an encoding symbol ID (ESI) is modified by an IoT device, so as to implicitly encrypt message data by preventing the correct reconstruction of data by an eavesdropper. In another example, the ESI is modified by an IoT device, so as to encrypt message data by preventing the correct reconstruction of data by an eavesdropper.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed: 1. An apparatus comprising a processor and a memory, the apparatus being connected to a network, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions stored in the memory of the apparatus which, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, cause the apparatus to perform operations comprising: determining an offset; modifying an encoded symbol identity (ESI) by the offset; encoding source packets in accordance with a fountain code and the modified ESI to construct output packets; and sending the output packets and the modified ESI to a receiver, such that the receiver can reconstruct the source packets from the output packets by using a key and the ESI. 2. The apparatus as recited in claim 1 , wherein determining the offset comprises using the key to generate the offset. 3. The apparatus as recited in claim 2, wherein the offset comprises a first offset and a second offset, the ESI comprises a sequence of values, and the apparatus further comprises computer-executable instructions that cause the apparatus to perform further operations comprising: based on the key, retrieving the first offset and the second offset from respective look-up tables; and applying the first offset the second offset to a first value and a second value, respectively, in the sequence of values. 4. The apparatus as recited in any one of the preceding claims, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions that cause the apparatus to perform further operations comprising: generating the key based on information specific to the apparatus, wherein the information changes with respect to time. 5. The apparatus as recited in any one of the preceding claims, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions that cause the apparatus to perform further operations comprising: 

 generating the key based on state information of a channel between the apparatus and the receiver. 6. An apparatus comprising a processor and a memory, the apparatus being connected to a network, the apparatus further comprising computer-executable instructions stored in the memory of the apparatus which, when executed by the processor of the apparatus, cause the apparatus to perform operations comprising: encoding source packets in accordance with a fountain code; and modifying how side information associated with the source packets is used by an intended receiver, such that a reconstruction probability of the source packets by an eavesdropper is reduced; and transmitting the source packets to the intended receiver. 7. The apparatus as recited in claim 6, the apparatus further comprising computer- executable instructions that cause the apparatus to perform further operations comprising: using a key that is shared with the intended receiver to modify how the side information is used by the intended receiver without modifying the side information. 8. A method performed by an IoT device, the method comprising: determining an offset; modifying an encoded symbol identity (ESI) by the offset; encoding source packets in accordance with a fountain code and the modified ESI to construct output packets; and sending the output packets and the modified ESI to a receiver, such that the receiver can reconstruct the source packets from the output packets by using a key and the ESI. 9. The method as recited in claim 8, wherein determining the offset comprises using the key to generate the offset. 10. The method as recited in claim 9, wherein the offset comprises a first offset and a second offset, the ESI comprises a sequence of values, and the method further comprises: based on the key, retrieving the first offset and the second offset from respective look-up tables; and 

 applying the first offset the second offset to a first value and a second value, respectively, in the sequence of values. 1 1. The method as recited in any one of claims 8 to 10, the method further comprising: generating the key based on information specific to the IoT device, wherein the information changes with respect to time. 12. The method as recited in any one of claims 8 to 11 , the method further comprising: generating the key based on state information of a channel between the apparatus and the receiver.
